message:
fix(http): deduplicate headers case-insensitively in RequestBuilder and build_request_bytes

Previously, calling .header("Authorization", value) followed by the
provider's own Authorization header (from api_key) would emit duplicate
headers in the raw HTTP request. This caused some API endpoints to
reject requests or behave unpredictably.

RequestBuilder.header() now performs case-insensitive lookup and replaces
any existing header with the same name instead of appending a duplicate.
The json() method now routes through header() instead of pushing directly,
so Content-Type also deduplicates correctly.

build_request_bytes() now:
- Skips Host, User-Agent, and Content-Length from the user-supplied
  headers list since these are always written canonically by the builder
- Allows User-Agent to be overridden: if the user headers contain a
  User-Agent entry, it takes precedence over the default
- Adds header_value() helper for reverse-searching the headers list

New helper: header_value() for O(n) reverse lookup by header name.

Tests added:
- RequestBuilder: header replaces case-insensitive duplicate names
- build_request_bytes: reserved headers are canonicalized (Host from URL,
  Content-Length from body, User-Agent from headers or default)
- cohere.rs: compat config Authorization header overrides api_key without
  duplicate; CapturedRequest gains header_lines + header_count()
- openai_responses.rs: asserts exactly 1 Authorization and 1 User-Agent
  header per request; CapturedRequest gains header_lines + header_count()
- Both provider test harnesses: parse_headers() now returns (HashMap,
  Vec<(name, value)>) to support duplicate-counting assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
